title: External Network Scan Job Failures and Timeout Trending

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 6.3.1 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 6.3.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.80: External Network Scan Job Failures and Timeout Trending.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.80' running on index=vm qualys:scan_summary or vendor API log qualys:scan_target, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=vm sourcetype="qualys:scan_summary" earliest=-180d
| where match(scan_title,"(?i)external|perimeter|PCI)")
| eval failed=if(match(status,"(?i)error|cancel|timeout|auth\\s*fail"),1,0)
| timechart span=30d sum(failed) as failed_jobs, count as jobs
| eval fail_rate=if(jobs>0, round(100*failed_jobs/jobs,2), null())
| trendline sma2(fail_rate) as fail_trend
description: Trends scan job timeouts and authentication failures so external vulnerability scanning remains reliable and gaps do not cause missed quarterly compliance windows.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes vm and sourcetypes qualys:scan_summary. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Deduplicate rescheduled runs by `scan_id`. (2) Correlate auth fails with credential vault rotations. (3) Alert when `fail_rate` exceeds 10%. (4) Document compensating manual scans if needed. Indexes required: `index=vm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=qualys:scan_summary`.
visualization: Line (`fail_rate`, `fail_trend`), column (`failed_jobs`), table (last error text truncated).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t sum(Vulnerabilities.cvss) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest span=30d | sort - agg_value
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for trends scan job timeouts and authentication failures so external vulnerability scanning remains reliable and gaps do not cause missed quarterly compliance windows. This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=vm` `qualys:scan_summary` or vendor API log `qualys:scan_target`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Deduplicate rescheduled runs by `scan_id`. (2) Correlate auth fails with credential vault rotations. (3) Alert when `fail_rate` exceeds 10%. (4) Document compensating manual scans if needed.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vm sourcetype="qualys:scan_summary" earliest=-180d
| where match(scan_title,"(?i)external|perimeter|PCI)")
| eval failed=if(match(status,"(?i)error|cancel|timeout|auth\\s*fail"),1,0)
| timechart span=30d sum(failed) as failed_jobs, count as jobs
| eval fail_rate=if(jobs>0, round(100*failed_jobs/jobs,2), null())
| trendline sma2(fail_rate) as fail_trend
```

Understanding this SPL

**External Network Scan Job Failures and Timeout Trending (PCI DSS Req 11.3.2, 6.3.1)** — Trends scan job timeouts and authentication failures so external vulnerability scanning remains reliable and gaps do not cause missed quarterly compliance windows.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vm` `qualys:scan_summary` or vendor API log `qualys:scan_target`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vm; **sourcetype**: qualys:scan_summary. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vm, sourcetype="qualys:scan_summary", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(scan_title,"(?i)external|perimeter|PCI)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **failed** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=30d** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **fail_rate** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(Vulnerabilities.cvss) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest span=30d | sort - agg_value
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**External Network Scan Job Failures and Timeout Trending (PCI DSS Req 11.3.2, 6.3.1)** — Trends scan job timeouts and authentication failures so external vulnerability scanning remains reliable and gaps do not cause missed quarterly compliance windows.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vm` `qualys:scan_summary` or vendor API log `qualys:scan_target`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: fail_rate, failed.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line (`fail_rate`, `fail_trend`), column (`failed_jobs`), table (last error text truncated).
